meaning: adj.地下的,地面下的,秘密的n.[英] 地铁adv.在地下,秘密地
examples: The miners were trapped deep underground.
The train was trapped underground by a fire.
The article 'Going underground at the Colosseum' was published in partnership with Lonely Planet.
The underground chapel contains the remains of around 4, 000 friars buried between 1500 and 1870.
